title: Floor-category variant for confirmed legacy block
summary: Refine the confirmed useful numeric floor-category block instead of removing it or adding unrelated features.
rationale: Removing numeric floor categories strongly hurt performance, so the block is useful. The next clean feature-engineering test is a small variant of that confirmed block, such as coarser buckets or rare-category clipping.
implementation_hint: Start from the parent two-seed RealMLP average code. Preserve the existing floor-category idea but change exactly one aspect: use round instead of floor, coarsen buckets with floor(x / 2), or clip rare floor categories to a shared rare value. Pick one variant only. Do not alter count encoding, target encoding, model parameters, or add new model families.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if the useful floor-category block can be made less noisy or more stable.
risk: The current floor transform may already be the best discretization, and changing it can lose threshold signal.
